architecture.summary: An iterative human–LLM co-design loop in which DeepSeek-R1 proposes, refines, and evaluates near-wall turbulence models. The LLM receives problem context, prior model evaluation results (a priori and a posteriori metrics), and human feedback, then generates new model equations and reasoning chains. The loop closes when the LLM's proposed models are tested against CFD data and results fed back for the next iteration. No trained neural components are central; the system is a structured prompting workflow with external CFD evaluation as the grounding engine.

biblio.title: Large language model driven development of turbulence models

claims.statement: The LLM proposes, refines and reasons about near-wall turbulence models under adverse pressure gradients (APGs), system rotation and surface roughness through multiple rounds of interaction involving long-chain reasoning and a priori and a posteriori evaluations.
The LLM generates models that not only rediscover established strategies, but also synthesise new ones that outperform baseline wall models.
The LLM recommends incorporating a material derivative to capture history effects in APG flows.
The LLM recommends modifying the law of the wall to account for system rotation.
The LLM develops rough-wall models informed by surface statistics.

The models developed are physically interpretable and grounded in clear reasoning, in contrast to conventional data-driven turbulence modelling often characterised by human-designed, black-box architectures.
